channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump opens up largest betting lead since days after Biden's dropout
item.description: Former President Trump has opened up a betting lead over Vice President Kamala Harris in the race for president of over 10 points for the first time since July 31.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Columbus Day Flashback: Harris excoriated European explorers for 'wave of devastation' to Native peoples
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris' 2021 comments claiming the U.S. must "not shy away" from it's "shameful past" of European explorers resurfaced on social media this election cycle.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Trump and Harris head back to Pennsylvania, the largest battleground state
item.description: Democrat Kamala Harris and Republican Donald Trump are taking their presidential campaigns back to Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Election 2024: Races to watch in New Jersey
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PX11) -- It's been one of the most notable years for New Jersey politics in recent memory — indictments, upsets, and loss are just some themes of what has been a year that could shape the Garden State's political future for years to come.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Biden warned Iran that killing Trump would be an act of war: report
item.description: President Biden reportedly instructed his National Security Council to make clear to Iran that any attempt on former President Trump’s life would be viewed as an act of war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrat Hakeem Jeffries stumps in California in a bid to reclaim House control
item.description: Democratic Leader Hakeem Jeffries crisscrossed Southern California over the weekend as his party works to wrest control of the U.S. House from Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Split-ticket voters could propel Gallego across finish line in Arizona
item.description: Split-ticket voters could ultimately propel Democratic Rep. Ruben Gallego to the Senate in Arizona even as voters in the Grand Canyon State choose former President Trump over Vice President Harris in the presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Georgia election workers move closer to collecting Rudy Giuliani’s assets
item.description: Two Georgia election workers are pushing to collect their $146 million defamation judgment against Rudy Giuliani, but the former New York City mayor is digging in amid an array of financial troubles.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Clyburn calls Project 2025 a 'Jim Crow 2.0'
item.description: Rep. James Clyburn (D-S.C.) on Sunday called the conservative policy agenda Project 2025 a “Jim Crow 2.0.” Clyburn said on CNN's "State of the Union" that he was the ninth Black lawmaker elected to Congress from South Carolina — almost 100 years after the previous Black lawmaker was elected.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's campaign crowdfunded millions online in an untraditional approach to emergency relief
item.description: Former President Donald Trump’s campaign is using GoFundMe to rally its donor network around the survivors of his July assassination attempt and Hurricane Helene’s destruction.
item.dc:creator: James Pollard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trends are good in the swing county GOP chair calls ‘Little Pennsylvania’: It’ll ‘be a repeat of ‘16’
item.description: Erie County, Pennsylvania, Republican Party Chair Tom Eddy spoke to Fox News Digital in a recent interview on his party's ground game, what he is seeing in his swing area and the importance of voting early.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate rankings: 5 seats most likely to flip
item.description: The battle for the Senate majority has hit its final month as Republicans race to end their four-year stint in the minority. The GOP needs to win only one of two competitive races in red states — Montana and Ohio — to do just that, barring a surprise loss elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Are political disagreements stressing you out? Here are tips to bridge the divide
item.description: With only weeks to a divisive election it can be hard to talk politics. Polarization can damage our relationships and our health. We have strategies to reduce election stress, starting with ourselves.
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey, MPR News

item.title: Trump's protests aside, his agenda has plenty of overlap with Project 2025
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Donald Trump insists that Project 2025, a nearly 1,000-page blueprint for a hard-right turn in American government and society, does not reflect his priorities for a White House encore. “I haven’t read it.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump tested the limits on using the military at home. If elected again, he plans to go further
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — During his first term as president, Donald Trump tested the limits of how he could use the military to achieve policy goals. If given a second term, the Republican and his allies are preparing to go much further, reimagining the military as an all-powerful tool to deploy on U.S.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump calls for adding 10,000 Border Patrol agents after derailing a bipartisan border bill
item.description: PRESCOTT VALLEY, Ariz. (AP) — Former President Donald Trump on Sunday proposed hiring 10,000 additional Border Patrol agents and giving them a $10,000 retention and signing bonus, after he derailed a bipartisan bill earlier this year that included funding for more border personnel.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump suggests a protester may get 'the hell knocked out of her' by her parents
item.description: COACHELLA, Calif. (AP) — Former President Donald Trump called for a protester at one of his rallies to “go back home to Mommy” to “get the hell knocked out of her,” his latest instance of using violent language when confronted by demonstrators.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walz tramps through tall grass but bags no birds as pheasant hunting season opens
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Democratic vice presidential candidate Tim Walz carried a shotgun and tramped through tall grass as Minnesota's pheasant hunting season opened, giving the campaign a chance to highlight the governor's rural roots and love of outdoor sports. Neither Walz nor Lt. Gov.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: S&P 500 a key predictor of the presidential race
item.description: The incumbent party kept the White House in 12 of the past 15 elections when the S&P 500 was positive ahead of Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Rebuilding Florida after Hurricane Milton and protecting against scams
item.description:
Recovery efforts can unite communities after catastrophic damage. Neighbors come together to help one another, while state officials and law enforcement work diligently to restore order. Gradually, residents begin to see their homes return to normalcy.
But even with the good, comes the bad.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Walz returns to Mankato to reprise role as coach, deliver football pep talk and campaign a little
item.description: The governor stopped through on a campaign visit marking the 25th anniversary of the season the Mankato West football team won the state championship with him as assistant coach. He will attend the Governors Pheasant opener in Sleepy Eye Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Henderson City Council candidate Dr. Monica Larson discusses government transparency, public safety, and housing
item.description: Henderson City Council candidate Dr. Monica Larson joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide Friday to discuss issues she believes need to be addressed in the city.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Battle to shore up bases remains as Election Day nears
item.description: MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst and his guest talks about deadly conflicts in the Middle East and how those have factored into domestic politics. Plus, we follow up with two Generation Z voters about how they’re approaching the election.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Matthew Alvarez, and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Michigan newspaper issues correction after Trump claims he won Man of Year award
item.description: A Michigan newspaper issued a correction after former President Donald Trump cited the paper at a speech in Detroit claiming he was Michigan’s “Man of the Year” in 2023.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Obama urges Black men to show up for Harris as he campaigns in critical Pennsylvania
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Former President Barack Obama gave a blistering critique of his White House successor Donald Trump and urged Black men to show up for Kamala Harris as he opened a swing-state tour for the Democratic ticket.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Shondra Summers-Armstrong discusses key issues in Las Vegas city council race
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) — Shondra Summers-Armstrong, currently serving as an Assemblywoman for District 6, is running for Las Vegas City Council, Ward 5.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pat Ryan 'incredibly frustrated' over lack of FEMA relief in Orange County
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- The race for New York’s 18th Congressional District heated up on Wednesday evening as candidates Alison Esposito and Pat Ryan faced off in a live debate on PIX11 News.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, PIX 11

item.title: Candidate Kamilah Bywaters discusses her vision for the Clark County School Board
item.description: Kamilah Bywaters, a candidate for the Clark County School District (CCSD) Board of Trustees, joined 8 News Now’s Daily Voting Guide to discuss her plans and priorities for District E, which covers Summerlin and northwest parts of the Las Vegas Valley.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News
